title: The Null-Point Awakening
content: [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'Where... am I? My head feels like it was hit by a freight train.' You open your eyes to a sky the color of a fresh bruise. You are lying in a glass-edged crater. The air is still, but you can feel a rhythmic pulsing deep in your bones. A flickering, gaseous light—a spirit fragment—hovers inches from your face. It is wounded, bleeding raw energy that smells like burnt copper and ozone. As your Earth-born logic tries to process the impossible, you feel a strange pull. The spirit isn't just near you; it's reacting to you. You are an anchor in this shifting reality.

choices.text: Reach out and stabilize the spirit's heat. (Thermal Entropy Affinity)
Anchor the spirit's erratic movement. (Vector Resonance Affinity)
Offer your own life-force to heal the flicker. (Biomorphic Flux Affinity)
Merge your mind with the spirit's sensory echo. (Cognitive Distortion Affinity)

title: The Scavenger's Shadow
content: The spirit merges into your chest with a silent, blinding flash. For a moment, you are the only thing that makes sense in this desert. [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'What was that? I feel... grounded. Different.' A figure—{npc:kaelen}—is peering over the edge of the crater now, their hand drifting toward a rusted iron knife. As they take in your {player.height}, {player.bodyType} frame and the strange, steady light now radiating from your chest, they [NPC_REACT]. 'I don't know what void you leaked out of,' the figure calls down, 'but you've sucked the life right out of the air. If you want a ride out of here, you're going to have to prove you aren't just a walking curse.'

choices.text: 'Who are you? And where am I?'
'Stay back. I'm not a curse, but I can be one if you touch that knife.'
(Ignore them and climb out of the crater yourself).

title: Arriving with {npc:kaelen}
content: The scavenger leads you through a gap in the outpost's reinforced perimeter. The guards nod as you pass, their eyes lingering on {npc:kaelen}'s knife rather than your strange arrival. {npc:kaelen} spits into the red dust. 'The Market is ahead. Find a merchant named Vane if you want to trade that light in your chest for something useful. Just don't get caught by the Syndicate patrols.'

title: Shadow of the Curse
content: {npc:kaelen} keeps a wary distance as you approach the outpost. 'You've got a sharp tongue and a strange light, stranger,' they mutter, 'but the Syndicate doesn't like loose ends. I'll get you inside, but after that, you're on your own.'

title: The Lonely Outpost
content: You arrive at the Borderlands Outpost alone, your feet heavy with red dust. Without a local to vouch for you, the iron gates feel more like a cage than a sanctuary. A Syndicate guard blocks your path, their rifle humming with thermal energy. 'No strangers without a permit. Unless you've got Shards or something to trade.'

choices.text: [Finesse 12] Sneak past through the ventilation ducts.

title: The Market Hub
content: The Outpost Market is a frantic web of commerce and desperation. Steam vents hiss, and the smell of ozone is constant. [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'This is where the world actually happens.' Merchants hawk fragments of technology, and scavengers recount tales of the deeper wastes. You are free to act here, but every action takes time.

choices.text: [SCAVENGE] Look for usable scrap in the outskirts.
[SOCIALIZE] Listen to the rumors in the tavern.

title: Syndicate Compliance Check
content: A {npc:syndicate_enforcer_grunt} blocks your path, their iron baton sparking with static energy. They look at the light pulsing in your chest with visible disgust. 'Newcomer, isn't it? You're leaking unregistered resonance. That's a Tier 1 infraction. Pay the 50 Shard Static-Levy now, or come with us to the Watch-Side Surplus for processing.'

choices.text: [Logic 12] Explain that your 'static' is actually a byproduct of a new energy-saving blueprint.

title: The Rusty Gear Tavern
content: You push through the heavy leather flaps of the local tavern. It smells of stale beer and burning sage. In the corner, a merchant is hawking curative salves. At the bar, a drunken engineer is rambling about the inefficiency of the local steam engines. [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'I could probably fix that engine if I showed them the Rankine cycle.'

choices.text: [Logic 12] Explain the Rankine Cycle to the engineer.

title: A Gravelly Warning
content: {npc:kaelen} looks up from their murky drink. 'You're making too much noise, {player.name}. The static you're dragging behind you is like a flare in the void. Captain Vane has already sent a search party for the source. If you don't find a way to mask that resonance, you'll meet Thorne sooner than you'd like.'

title: The Captain's Summons
content: Two Syndicate Enforcers corner you outside the market. 'Captain Vane requests your presence at the Iron Watch HQ. He doesn't like it when anomalies wander his territory without an audit. Don't make this difficult.'

title: The Glass Desert
content: The walk to the outpost is a test of endurance. The red dust clogs your lungs, and the rhythmic pulsing of the world is deafening in the silence. [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'If this is the world now, I need to adapt fast.' You pass the skeletal remains of a steam-crawler, its brass gears partially fused by entropy. You can feel the residue of a massive energy discharge here.

choices.text: Keep moving. The outpost is in sight.

title: Scavenging Results
content: You spend hours combing the outskirts of the market. The ground is littered with 'Ghost-Static'—the psychic residue of forgotten memories. You manage to find: {game.lastLoot}. [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'It's not much, but it's a start.'

title: A Resonance Realized
content: You focus on the {player.blessedSkill}. As you apply your understanding of systemic resonance, the 'warmth' in your marrow suddenly crystallizes into a name and a function. [INNER_MONOLOGUE] 'It's not just heat. It's a signature. I can... stabilize the air around me.'
